biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:STABLE — 7/10 measurable units; 29d7d6be-2 (cd+ls+find+wc-l+rg via Bash), 29d7d6be-3 (find+grep pipeline via Bash), 29d7d6be-6 (cat>> append via Bash instead of Edit/Write), 29d7d6be-24 (grep/sed via Bash multiple calls), 29d7d6be-25 (grep+sed+head+python3-file-write via Bash), bc2715fa-5 (cd+rg x4 via Bash), bc2715fa-7 (rg on nonexistent path via Bash); rate ~58-70% of measurable units, within historical 55-65% band but at upper end; python3 inline file-write in 29d7d6be-25 is a new variant bypassing Write tool entirely via Bash subprocess

path_assumption_bias:RECURRING:STABLE — 2 units matching prior cycle stable plateau of 2; bc2715fa-5: cd frontend && rg fails 4 consecutive times before pwd recovery; bc2715fa-7: rg on nonexistent path fails before pivot; identical recovery topology (wrong relative path → multiple failures → pwd+ls → corrected retry); count at 2 matches stable baseline, no worsening

optimistic_edit_without_read:RECURRING:STABLE — 2 units; 29d7d6be-24: Edit widgets-v2.md fail then Read then Edit success, Edit process-vs-instance-math.md fail then Read then Edit success, Edit worker-v2-but-better.md fail then Read then Edit success (3 sub-instances within one unit); 29d7d6be-25: Edit route-vs-server-action.md fail then python3 Write-escape (bypasses Edit entirely); co-occurrence with re_edit_thrash in unit 24 confirms shared root cause; unit-level count 2 matches prior stable plateau

re_edit_thrash:RECURRING:STABLE — 1 unit; 29d7d6be-24: 3 distinct Edit fail-read-succeed cycles across 3 different files within a single unit (widgets-v2.md, process-vs-instance-math.md, worker-v2-but-better.md); count at 1 unit below prior worsening peak of 5 units — improvement signal but single session sample is insufficient to declare resolved

redundant_read_same_unit:RECURRING:STABLE — 1 unit; 29d7d6be-2: Read product-todos.md x2 with no intervening edit or offset variation between duplicate reads; count at 1 matches or is below prior stable plateau of 2; no worsening

python3_file_write_via_bash_bypass:NEW — unit 29d7d6be-25: python3 inline script (p.write_text()) used via Bash to bulk-inject frontmatter into 10 files, bypassing Write and Edit tools entirely; not classified as shell_tool_preference_violation because bulk-file operations lack a dedicated tool equivalent, but Write tool was available for individual files; the pattern is distinct from prior instances: no Edit-fail-before-Write-escape topology, just direct bulk-write via subprocess; first measured instance; ambiguous whether appropriate (bulk efficiency) or violation (bypasses audit trail and permission model of Write tool)
recommendations: python3_file_write_via_bash_bypass is NEW — assess whether bulk file writes via python3 subprocess are categorically acceptable or should require Write tool per file; prior sessions have no ruling on this variant; if the answer is 'Write tool required', this becomes shell_tool_preference_violations sub-variant; if 'bulk-python is ok', document the carve-out to prevent false positives in future calibration cycles

shell_tool_preference_violations is at upper end of historical band (70%) for this batch despite being STABLE overall — no new recommendation given prior cycles already flag this; pattern has not worsened meaningfully

optimistic_edit_without_read and re_edit_thrash show improvement signals (unit counts below prior worsening peaks) but single-session sample; withhold improvement classification until confirmed across 2+ sessions

path_assumption_bias stable at 2 — no change from prior recommendation; both instances are bc2715fa session (older), not 29d7d6be session; the zero-instance new-session signal from 29d7d6be suggests possible improvement in newer sessions
